hero.tagline: Software for passionate enthusiasts who want to explore motorsport, aviation, and simulation hobbies without a punishing learning curve or professional-grade cost.

services.title: What We Build
services.subtitle: GPS makes STEM and software tools for enthusiasts in motorsport, aviation, and other specialized hobbies, with an emphasis on lowering cost, time, and learning barriers.

items.web_projects_desc: Building interactive tools, dashboards, and browser experiences for niche enthusiast communities.
items.mobile_experiments_desc: Prototyping mechanics and companion tools that help users understand complex hobbies faster.
items.open_source_desc: Designing software that makes hobby ecosystems easier to enter, study, and share.
items.hackathons_desc: Turning technical ideas into usable products quickly so enthusiasts can test them in the real world.
items.creative_coding_desc: Creating logic, economies, and simulations tuned for serious hobby communities.
items.learning_together_desc: Helping passionate players and makers learn together instead of facing a steep barrier alone.

story.title: Our Story
story.subtitle: Founded in 2025, GPS connects F1 fan culture, civil aviation curiosity, tabletop strategy design, and browser-based simulation into a focused playground for technically minded hobbyists.

milestone1.title: The Spark
milestone1.desc: Started as individuals working on personal projects. School brought us together.

milestone2.title: Board Game Origins
milestone2.desc: Formed the team and began developing board games after class.

milestone3.title: Digital Shift
milestone3.desc: Ventured into computerized simulation and webapps, embracing 'vibe-coding' with Trae.

milestone4.title: Official Launch
milestone4.desc: Setting up our backend server and launching the official website. Expanding from our Bilibili community to the world.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.content: We are F1 fans, aviation enthusiasts, student developers, and game designers building from China for a wider community of technically minded hobbyists. GPS is for people who want better software to explore motorsport, civil aviation, strategy systems, and other high-barrier hobbies without paying professional-level cost, time, or learning overhead.
mission.quote: We turn hard-to-enter hobbies into software that feels welcoming, usable, and worth coming back to.
mission.author: The GPS Team

products.title: Hard-Core Mechanisms
products.subtitle: Computerized simulations and boardgames focused on aviation and motorsports. No flashy UI—just pure functionality.

flight_sim.name: Box This Lap
flight_sim.description: F1 constructor championship game focused on race-weekend decisions and points maximization.
flight_sim.mechanism: Constructor strategy drafting, budget-risk balancing, dynamic scoring based on race outcomes.

race_strategy.name: DeltaDash
race_strategy.description: Officially released open-wheel boardgame with delicate strategy management and memes.
race_strategy.mechanism: Sophisticated strategy management, resource luck-press, expansion compatibility, and live community payout competition.

telemetry.name: Telemetry Analyzer
telemetry.description: Tool for analyzing racing telemetry data to optimize lap times.
telemetry.mechanism: Data ingestion pipeline, suspension travel analysis, cornering G-force mapping, throttle/brake trace overlay.

atc_sim.name: Terminal Control
atc_sim.description: Air Traffic Control simulation for high-density terminal airspace.
atc_sim.mechanism: Aircraft separation algorithms, wake turbulence modeling, RNAV approach pathing, conflict detection logic.

skyline_tragedy.name: Skyline Tragedy
skyline_tragedy.description: 6-DOF simulation with comprehensive flight initialization and parameter configuration.
skyline_tragedy.mechanism: 6-DOF physics, SSO integration, difficulty scaling, NPC crew intelligence.

reaction.title: F1 Reaction Test
reaction.desc: Test your reaction time against the lights. Can you beat an F1 driver?

pit_stop.title: Pit Stop Challenge
pit_stop.desc: Change tyres as fast as you can!
pit_stop.instructions: Drag tyres to change. Hold mouse to loosen/tighten bolts.
pit_stop.step1_desc: Hold on the red nut until it turns green.
pit_stop.step2_desc: Drag the old tyre away, then drag in a new one.
pit_stop.step3_desc: Hold again to secure the wheel.

gt_pit_stop.title: GT/Rally Pit Stop
gt_pit_stop.desc: Open door, pull driver out, jack the car, change 4 tyres with bolts, clean up, and manually start.
gt_pit_stop.instructions: Drag door, driver, and rear storage; drag jack and toggle lever to green; hold to unscrew/secure bolts; drag new tyres from storage; lower jack (red), return jack/tyres; get back in, close the door, then Manual Start.
gt_pit_stop.objective: Complete the full GT/Rally pit procedure and change all 4 tyres.
gt_pit_stop.step1_desc: Open door, pull driver out, open rear storage, take jack, raise to green.
gt_pit_stop.step2_desc: Unscrew bolts, remove old tyres, install new tyres from storage, secure bolts.
gt_pit_stop.step3_desc: Lower jack (red), return jack & tyres, get back in, close door, Manual Start.

policies.market_content:
Our market allows you to trade tickets and support projects.

Prices fluctuate based on demand. High risk items like Driver Bets can result in total loss.

Always do your own research before investing.
policies.token_rep_content:
Tokens are our internal currency for trading and rewards.

Reputation reflects your contribution and trustworthiness. High reputation unlocks advanced features like creating campaigns.
policies.mission_content:
Complete missions to earn Tokens and Reputation.

Submissions are reviewed by admins. Quality work is rewarded; spam or low-effort submissions will be rejected.
policies.betting_content: Betting involves risk. Driver Bets are binary outcomes (Win/Lose).
policies.fees_content: A 2% fee applies to all market transactions to support the platform.
policies.minigame_content: Minigames are for entertainment. Spamming or using bots to farm rewards is prohibited and will result in a ban.

telemetry.title: Live Telemetry Analysis
telemetry.subtitle: Powered by OpenF1 API. In-memory cache enabled.
telemetry.select_driver_prompt: Please select at least one driver from the sidebar.

hud.no_data: No telemetry stream is available for this driver in the selected session.

lobby.no_rooms: No open rooms. Create one to start!

actions.strategist_help: Strategist reads the selected car. If it looks normal, your plan also protects it this race.

night.hint_tc: Select a driver from the grid to {{action}}.
night.hint_tp: TP+ sees every player's role without side. TP- selects a player to eject.
night.hint_is: Choose Sense or Eject, then select one player. You cannot do both in one night.
night.hint_st: Select a driver from the grid to {{action}}.
night.no_action: You have no night action this round. Wait for others to complete their actions.

rules.rule_1: Players are referenced as #1, #2, #3... in public discussion.
rules.rule_2: Role codes: TP = Team Principal, TC = Technician, IS = Inspector, ST = Strategist.
rules.rule_3: Alignment codes: + = positive, - = negative. Example: #4 TC- means #4 is a bad Technician.
rules.rule_4: Positive wins by firing all negative players; negative wins by causing team expulsion.
rules.rule_5: Each round: night actions → race → discussion → voting

log.title: Game Log
log.placeholder: Type commands like /sus 4 IS- (atk 2) (clean). Tab accepts, ↑↓ selects.
log.click_to_vote: Click a player number card to vote, or lock with no target to abstain.
log.abstain_hint: No target selected: you will abstain.
log.bot_vote_locked: I am locking this vote on #{{number}}.
log.bot_vote_abstain: I am abstaining because the vote case is not strong enough yet.
log.human_vote_locked: I am locking my vote on #{{number}}.
log.driver_dnf_debrief: From the cockpit, it felt like {{cause}}. Confidence: {{uncertainty}}.
log.driver_finish_clean: Clean race from my side. Car felt normal.
log.driver_finish_suspicious: I finished, but something felt off during the race.
log.strategist_normal: Your read on Driver {{driver}}: the car looks normal. You protected that car this race.
log.strategist_abnormal: Your read on Driver {{driver}}: something does not look normal. You got information, but could not safely protect it.
log.strategist_sabotage: You sabotaged Driver {{driver}} with bad strategy this race.
log.principal_eject_result: You ejected #{{number}} ({{role}}) as TP- during the night.
log.driver_reflection: From the cockpit, it felt like {{cause}}. Confidence: {{uncertainty}}.
log.template_help: Templates: combine intent, target, reason, and certainty.
log.template_1: I suspect #{{number}} because the race result looked coordinated.
log.template_2: I trust #{{number}} for now; their claim sounds consistent.
log.template_3: I need #{{number}} to explain their read before I vote.
log.template_4: I do not have enough evidence, so I may abstain this round.
log.bot_clean_1: Clean race. I am not rushing a vote.
log.bot_clean_2: Both cars finished, so I want more discussion first.
log.bot_clean_3: Low pressure round. I am keeping notes.
log.bot_one_dnf_1: One DNF is enough to compare stories.
log.bot_one_dnf_2: That race looked off. I want explanations.
log.bot_one_dnf_3: One car dropped out, so reads matter now.
log.bot_two_dnf_1: Two DNFs changes everything. We need accusations.
log.bot_two_dnf_2: That was a disaster. I am looking for patterns.
log.bot_two_dnf_3: Two cars out means sabotage is likely.
log.bot_driver_ref_1: Driver {{driver}} reported issues. Someone was responsible for that car.
log.bot_driver_ref_2: Driver {{driver}} mentioned problems. I want to know who handled that strategy.
log.bot_both_dnf_1: Both drivers reported issues. That is not coincidence.
log.bot_both_dnf_2: Both cars had problems. Someone sabotaged both or failed protection.
log.bot_suspect_1: #{{number}} feels suspicious, but I am not locked in.
log.bot_suspect_2: I want #{{number}} to talk before I decide.
log.bot_suspect_3: My read is leaning toward #{{number}}, unless someone has a better case.
log.bot_response_1: I heard that. #{{number}} is possible, but I want more than one claim.
log.bot_response_2: If we are looking at #{{number}}, I need to compare that with the race result.
log.bot_response_3: #{{number}} is on my list too, but I could abstain if this stays weak.
log.bot_uncertain_1: I do not have enough to make a confident vote.
log.bot_uncertain_2: That does not settle it for me yet.
log.bot_uncertain_3: I may give up my vote if nobody has a stronger read.
log.bot_role_claim: I am claiming {{role}}, so I want the race result compared against role claims before we fire anyone.
log.bot_action_claim: My night action was to {{action}} Driver {{driver}}, so that result should matter in the vote.
log.bot_is_eject_claim: I ejected #{{number}} because my result says {{role}}.
log.bot_evaluation_race: #{{number}} fits the race evidence best. Confidence: {{confidence}}.
log.bot_evaluation_claim: #{{number}} has the claim that worries me most. Confidence: {{confidence}}.
log.bot_evaluation_pressure: #{{number}} is benefiting from the table pressure too cleanly. Confidence: {{confidence}}.
log.bot_evaluation_vote: #{{number}}'s vote path is the part I dislike most. Confidence: {{confidence}}.
log.bot_evaluation_uncertain: #{{number}} is only my softest lead. Confidence: {{confidence}}.
log.bot_disproven_push: #{{number}} pushed a case that flipped positive, so my old read has to reverse hard.
log.template_hint: Command manual: /action parameters (reason). Main examples: /trust 5 (checked), /sus 4 IS- (atk 2) (clean), /claim ST+ (checked), /action protected d1 (strong). Brackets add reasons/certainty; Enter sends only valid commands.
log.command_manual: Format: /main parameters (reason). Use player numbers 1-6, role+side like IS- or ST+, and bracket notes like (checked), (clean), (atk 2), (strong).
log.command_hint_start: Use /sus, /trust, /ask, /claim, or /action; add bracket notes like (checked) or (atk 2).
log.template_intent_logic: My logic centers on {{role}} and Driver {{driver}}.
log.template_intent_attack: The attack on #{{number}} is that they fit {{role}}.
log.template_intent_vote: I can vote #{{number}} as suspected negative.
log.template_intent_world: A world with #{{number}} as {{role}} changes Driver {{driver}}.
log.template_intent_ig: Based on the evidence, {{role}} must exist in this game.
log.template_intent_nig: Based on the evidence, {{role}} cannot exist in this game.
log.template_reason_anomalous_attack: #{{number}} attacked a low-suspicion target while others focused elsewhere.
log.template_reason_speech_evasion: #{{number}} has not made any concrete claim yet.
log.template_reason_def_anomalous_attack: #{{number}}'s accusation targets someone with low suspicion while ignoring the real pattern.
log.template_reason_def_speech_evasion: #{{number}} has not claimed a role yet, so their accusation carries no weight.
log.template_reason_race_dnf: A DNF needs an action explanation.
log.template_reason_race_clean: A clean finish weakens the sabotage case.
log.template_reason_claim_action: Their action claim is the key point.
log.template_reason_claim_contradiction: Their claim path does not line up.
log.template_reason_role_tp: A TP claim has no night action to verify.
log.template_reason_role_tc: A TC claim should explain protection or sabotage around a driver.
log.template_reason_role_is: An IS claim should produce a player identity check.
log.template_reason_role_st: An ST claim should explain driver analysis or strategy impact.
log.template_reason_role_mismatch: The claimed role and action path do not fit together.
log.template_reason_driver_signal: The driver report changes which role worlds are plausible.
log.template_reason_counterclaim: The counterclaim means one of these worlds is probably false.
log.template_reason_claim_pressure: The claim is being used to shape the vote, so credibility matters.
log.template_reason_protective_claim: A protection claim should line up with whether damage was prevented.
log.template_reason_sus_race_dnf: Driver {{driver}} had a DNF, so a negative technical or strategy role is plausible.
log.template_reason_sus_race_clean: Driver {{driver}} looked clean, so this accusation needs a non-race basis.
log.template_reason_sus_claim_contradiction: Their role claim and action claim do not match.
log.template_reason_sus_role_tc: Technical issues point more toward TC logic than ST logic.
log.template_reason_sus_role_is: An unexpected eject or identity result points toward IS logic.
log.template_reason_sus_role_st: Strategic problems point more toward ST logic than TC logic.
log.template_reason_sus_role_mismatch: I suspect #{{number}} because the claimed role does not explain the claimed action.
log.template_reason_sus_driver_signal: The driver signal points toward a negative action world, not a positive read.
log.template_reason_sus_counterclaim: This counterclaim pair cannot both be clean without another explanation.
log.template_reason_trust_claim_role: A positive claim lowers suspicion only if the action path stays consistent.
log.template_reason_trust_driver_signal: The driver signal makes this claim more believable for now.
log.template_reason_trust_protective_claim: If protection explains the race, this claim deserves temporary trust.
log.template_reason_vote_race_dnf: The vote is about who could be negative after Driver {{driver}} failed.
log.template_reason_vote_claim_contradiction: The vote follows a public contradiction, not a role guess.
log.template_reason_vote_vote_pressure: The vote pressure is useful only if it points to a likely negative.
log.template_reason_vote_uncertain: I can vote here, but the case is still not role-confirmed.
log.template_reason_attack_race_dnf: The race result gives a concrete negative-action angle.
log.template_reason_attack_claim_contradicti: The strongest attack is the mismatch between claimed role and claimed action.
log.template_reason_attack_vote_pressure: The pressure pattern looks coordinated enough to question.
log.template_reason_explain_claim_role: A role claim without side is not enough; the action path must fit too.
log.template_reason_explain_claim_action: The action claim needs a target and a race result attached to it.
log.template_reason_explain_claim_contradict: The explanation should resolve the contradiction before we vote.
log.template_reason_switch_vote_pressure: I can move if the stronger negative case is on this target.
log.template_reason_switch_uncertain: This is a tentative switch, not a hard role read.
log.template_reason_logic_race_dnf: The logic starts from the DNF and works back to possible negative actions.
log.template_reason_logic_race_clean: A clean race means the logic should focus on claims rather than damage.
log.template_reason_action_claim_action: The action claim should be checked against the driver result.
log.template_reason_action_race_dnf: The DNF makes this action claim important to verify.
log.template_reason_world_role_is: If #{{number}} was fired outside the vote, an IS- or TP- world must be considered.
log.template_reason_world_role_tc: If there are technical failures without strategy signs, TC- is the cleaner world.
log.template_reason_world_role_st: If the driver report sounds strategic, ST- is the cleaner world.
log.template_reason_def_race_clean: A clean finish makes the case against #{{number}} weaker.
log.template_reason_def_claim_action: Their action claim gives a testable explanation.
log.template_certainty_weak: This is only a weak read for now.
log.template_certainty_strong: I am confident enough to vote on it.
log.step_choose_action_target: Choose an action and target next.
log.step_append_optional: Now send, or append role/reason/certainty modules.
log.template_5: I claim {{role}}. If #{{number}} is telling the truth, then someone else must explain the race action order.
log.template_6: Why is #{{number}} so certain? That confidence itself looks suspicious unless they have a night result.
log.template_7: I want to compare claims before voting. Who inspected a player, protected a driver, or touched Driver {{driver}}?
log.template_8: My vote is not locked. I can switch if someone connects a role action to the DNF pattern.
log.template_9: If Driver {{driver}} was clean, then the strongest accusation should be against a false action claim, not a random target.
log.template_10: If Driver {{driver}} was sabotaged but survived, protection probably changed the outcome; that matters before voting.
log.template_11: I need the inspector claim to say whether #{{number}} is consistent with their public role claim.
log.template_12: A good vote needs three pieces: the role claim, the night action, and the race result.
log.bot_defense_1: #{{accuser}} is forcing a weak case on me. I claim {{role}} and that push is suspicious.
log.bot_defense_2: No, #{{accuser}} is overstating it. My claim is {{role}}, and I want their reason clearly logged.
log.bot_defense_3: #{{accuser}} accused me too quickly. I am {{role}} claim, and I question why they need a fast vote.
log.bot_counter_1: If #{{accuser}} is wrong about #{{accused}}, we should ask what #{{accuser}} gains from that.
log.bot_counter_2: I do not fully buy the case on #{{accused}} yet. #{{accuser}} needs to explain the timing.
log.bot_counter_3: #{{accused}} should answer, but #{{accuser}} is also creating pressure very early.
log.bot_ack_1: That gives me a cleaner read on #{{number}}.
log.bot_ack_2: I will keep #{{number}} in my notes, but I am not rushing.
log.bot_ack_3: The case on #{{number}} is plausible, not confirmed.
log.bot_explosion_1: #{{number}} just made an openly negative claim. That is not normal town behavior.
log.bot_explosion_2: If #{{number}} really said sabotage, that is the strongest public evidence we have.
log.bot_suspicious_claim_1: Why is #{{number}} suspecting someone of being positive? That's backwards logic.
log.bot_suspicious_claim_2: #{{number}}'s claim doesn't make sense. Positive players hunt negatives, not other positives.

manual.title: How to Play
manual.overview: F1 Deduction is a social deduction game set in a Formula 1 team. Positive players try to complete the season without being sabotaged; negative players try to cause enough damage to get the team fired.
manual.phase_night: Each player performs their role action in secret. Negative players coordinate sabotage; positive players protect or investigate.
manual.phase_discussion: All players discuss race results, make claims, and accuse or defend each other. Use the template prompt to build structured statements.
manual.phase_voting: Players vote to fire one person. The player with the most votes is eliminated. Bots speak while voting and their live vote counts are visible.
manual.phase_race: The race resolves based on night actions. DNFs increase board pressure. If pressure reaches the threshold, the board fires someone.
manual.role_TP_pos: Team Principal (positive): Sees every player's role at night (without side). No direct action.
manual.role_TP_neg: Team Principal (negative): Ejects one player at night. Knows teammates' roles.
manual.role_TC_pos: Technician (positive): Protects one driver each night, reducing DNF chance.
manual.role_TC_neg: Technician (negative): Sabotages one driver each night, increasing DNF chance.
manual.role_IS_pos: Inspector (positive): Each night, choose to either sense one player's role or eject one player. Cannot do both.
manual.role_IS_neg: Inspector (negative): Each night, choose to either sense one player's role or eject one player. Cannot do both.
manual.role_ST_pos: Strategist (positive): Analyzes a driver; if normal, also protects them this race.
manual.role_ST_neg: Strategist (negative): Applies bad strategy to sabotage a driver.
manual.bot_overview: Bots only use public information: race results, public claims, and discussion messages. They cannot see hidden roles or alignments.
manual.bot_scoring: Bots score each target using: base suspicion, DNF count, explosive claims (self-declared negative or sabotage), public claim contradictions (role vs action mismatch), and abnormal protection patterns.
manual.bot_voting: Bots align their vote with their highest-scored non-teammate target. Negative bots protect teammates unless the teammate is already publicly exposed.
manual.bot_speech: When a bot has a suspect, it picks from accusation intents (sus, attack, explain, vote, switch). Without a suspect, it picks from claim/defense intents. Bots never accuse and defend the same target in the same turn.
